content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
May 7, 2014 
Kimberly D. Willy, Tax Counsel m 
Administrative Oversight Section (MIC: 82) 
Settlement & Taxpayer Services Division 
Board of Equalization Legal Department 
450 N Street 
Sacramento CA 95814 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-14-064 
Dear Ms. Willy: 
This letter responds to your request for additional advice on behalf of the Board of 
Equalization ("BOE") Chairman Jerome E. Horton regarding mass mailing provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 In your original advice request ( Willy Advice Letter, A-14-
008), you asked and we advised as follows: 
(1) Would the mass mailing restrictions in Section 89001 and Regulation 18901 apply to 
the posters that reference both Mr. Horton and another BOE member? 
Yes. All the criteria in Regulation 18901 would be met if a total of more than 200 of 
either poster are delivered by any means to recipients at their residence, place of employment, 
business or post office box and/or non-governmental business locations. 
(2) Would Chairman Horton need to count the 25-30 posters that will be mailed featuring 
the other BOE member towards the 200 mass mailing limitation? 
No. The reference to Chairman Horton in the other BOE member's poster would fall 
under the "letterhead" exemption. Therefore, the other BOE member's poster would not count 
towards Mr. Horton's 200 mass mailing limitation. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. unless otherwise indicated. 


In your Jetter of April 2, 2014, you provided us with additional facts: 
File No. A-14-064 
Page No. 2 
• Chairman Horton's name was added to the poster, with Member Two's 
permission, because the Chairman, along with other elected officials, was invited 
to the Voluntary Income Tax Assistance program ("VITA") and planned to attend 
and provide welcoming remarks to kick off the event in Member Two's district. 
• 
In a "revised, redacted copy" of the poster you include with your latest request, 2 
you state that Member Two's name and title appear above the Chairman's and in 
the same size and font as that of the Chairman's name and title. 
You pose an additional question regarding the "official agency event'" exception and 
whether it would apply Lo either of the two posters. In the Willy Advice Letter, A-14-008, we 
concluded the exception does not apply to either of the posters you included because: 
(I) Chairman Horton's posters included more than one reference to him and included a 
photograph of him; and 
(2) Member Two's poster also included more than one reference to Member Two and 
included the member's name, title and photograph. 
While your new facts do not change the original conclusions, the new facts do support 
application of "official agency event" exception to the use of the Chairman's name in Member 
Two's posters. You clarified that the Chairman had planned a visit and welcoming remarks at 
the VITA kick off meeting at Member Two's district. This would be directly related to the 
Chairman's governmental duties and the meeting would be held jointly by Member Two and the 
Chairman, and that BOE is providing use of its facilities for the event. Therefore, it falls under 
the exception in Regulation 18901(b)(9)(A) which provides an exception to the Act's mass 
mailing restrictions for "[a]n announcement of any meeting or event of the type listed in 
paragraphs 1 or 2." 
"1. An announcement sent to an elected officer's constituents concerning a public 
meeting which is directly related to the elected officer's incumbent governmental 
duties, which is to be held by the elected officer, and w/zich the elected officer 
intends to attend. 
"2. An announcement of any official agency event or events for which the agency 
is providing the use of its facilities or staff or other financial support. 
2 We note that the revised, redacted copy of the poster you provided appears different from the copy of the 
redacted poster we reviewed in your prior request for advice, Willy Advice letter, A-14-008 (and may have been 
updated in response to our lener). We have focused solely on the latest revised poster for purposes of this analysis. 


ER:jgl 
File No. A-14-064 
Page No. 3 
"(B) Any announcement provided for in this subdivision (b)(9) shall not 
include the elected officer's photograph or signature and may include only a 
single mention of the elected officer's name except as permitted elsewhere in this 
regulation." 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
-
By: 
Emelyn Rodriguez 
Counsel, Legal Divis'on

sections.question: Would the 'official agency event' exception in Regulation 18901(b)(9) apply to posters that reference both Board of Equalization Chairman Jerome E. Horton and another BOE member in connection with a Voluntary Income Tax Assistance (VITA) program kick-off event?
sections.conclusion: Yes. The new facts support application of the 'official agency event' exception to the use of the Chairman's name in Member Two's posters because the Chairman had planned a visit and welcoming remarks at the VITA kick-off meeting in Member Two's district, directly related to his governmental duties, the meeting would be held jointly by both members, and BOE is providing facilities for the event. This falls under Regulation 18901(b)(9)(A), provided the announcement does not include the elected officer's photograph or signature and includes only a single mention of the officer's name.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks whether the official agency event exception to mass mailing restrictions would apply to posters featuring both Chairman Horton and another BOE member for a jointly-held VITA program event where the Chairman planned to attend and provide welcoming remarks.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the official agency event exception applies to Chairman Horton's inclusion in Member Two's posters because the VITA event was directly related to his governmental duties, jointly held, and supported by BOE facilities. However, the exception requires that any such announcement exclude the officer's photograph and signature and contain only a single mention of the officer's name.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a follow-up advice letter referencing a prior advice letter (A-14-008). The document lacks traditional section headers for QUESTION and CONCLUSION, but both are clearly present in the body text. The letter discusses three separate questions/conclusions about mass mailing restrictions and the official agency event exception.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Would the 'official agency event' exception in Regulation 18901(b)(9) apply to posters that reference both Board of Equalization Chairman Jerome E. Horton and another BOE member in connection with a Voluntary Income Tax Assistance (VITA) program kick-off event?

CONCLUSION: Yes. The new facts support application of the 'official agency event' exception to the use of the Chairman's name in Member Two's posters because the Chairman had planned a visit and welcoming remarks at the VITA kick-off meeting in Member Two's district, directly related to his governmental duties, the meeting would be held jointly by both members, and BOE is providing facilities for the event. This falls under Regulation 18901(b)(9)(A), provided the announcement does not include the elected officer's photograph or signature and includes only a single mention of the officer's name.

embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter responds to a follow-up request regarding mass mailing restrictions under the Political Reform Act, specifically addressing whether the 'official agency event' exception applies to posters featuring both BOE Chairman Horton and another member for a VITA program. The FPPC concludes the exception does apply based on the event's connection to governmental duties and joint agency sponsorship, subject to restrictions on photography, signature, and mention limitations.
